sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) desire, regret. 2Ch 21:20, בְּלֹא חֶמְדָּה “he departed regretted by no one.”

(2) that which is desired, delight. 1Sa 9:20 Dan 11:37, חֶמְדַּת נָשִׁים “the delight of women;” this is to be understood, as the context shews it must, of some idol, especially worshipped by the Syrian women, such as Astarte, or Anaitis.

(3) pleasantness, excellence. אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה the pleasant land, Jer 3:19 Eze 26:12. כְּלֵי חֶמְדָּה precious jewels, 2Ch 32:27 36:10 .
